name: Vikramaditya
description: Vikramaditya, a legendary emperor of ancient India, is celebrated for his wisdom, courage, and justice. While his historical existence is debated, he remains a prominent figure in Indian folklore and literary traditions. Stories about Vikramaditya depict him as a righteous ruler who travelled widely, met various supernatural beings, and solved complex disputes with his unparalleled intellect. He is often associated with Ujjain and is believed to have ruled during the first century BCE or CE. The 'Vikrama Samvat' calendar era is attributed to him, marking his victory over the Saka invaders. Tales of his court, including the 'Singhasan Battisi' (Thirty-two Tales of the Throne) and the 'Vetala Panchavimshati' (Twenty-five Tales of the Vampire), showcase his ability to discern truth from falsehood and his unwavering commitment to dharma. Vikramaditya's reign is considered a golden age of justice, prosperity, and cultural flourishing. He embodies the ideal of a benevolent king, a protector of the weak, and a champion of righteousness. His legacy continues to inspire generations with his tales of valor, wisdom, and unwavering adherence to moral principles, making him a timeless hero in Hindu mythology and a symbol of ideal kingship.
subtitle: The Wise and Just King
achievements: Presiding over a golden age of justice
Protecting the weak and upholding Dharma

keyMyths.title: The Singhasan Battisi
keyMyths.description: The Singhasan Battisi recounts the stories of 32 celestial nymphs who guarded the throne of Vikramaditya, each narrating a tale of his exceptional virtue and justice. These stories reveal the qualities that made him a just and beloved ruler.

keyMyths.title: The Vetala Panchavimshati
keyMyths.description: Vikramaditya's encounters with the Vetala involve him answering riddles posed by the vampire, testing his wisdom and knowledge. He has to maintain silence while carrying the Vetala's corpse, lest the vampire escapes.

keyMyths.title: Victory over the Shakas
keyMyths.description: Vikramaditya is credited with defeating the Shakas, foreign invaders, and establishing a new era known as the Vikrama Samvat to commemorate his victory. This event solidified his reputation as a powerful and capable ruler.

sources.author: Somadeva
